note: Daily supplement protocol tailored to Vedant. v2 adds shift-work specific tier (melatonin, magnesium glycinate, L-theanine, ashwagandha, glycine) — see SUPPLEMENTS.md for evidence. Ashwagandha removed — contraindicated with user's SSRI medication.

supplements.name: Creatine monohydrate
supplements.purpose: Strength + muscle gain. Vegetarians respond MORE than omnivores.
supplements.sideEffects: Mildly raises serum creatinine on blood tests — tell your GP you supplement.
supplements.notes: Skip loading phase. Stir into water, coffee, or shake.

supplements.name: Whey protein
supplements.purpose: Convenience tool to hit 130-140g daily protein target.
supplements.notes: Only what you need to close the protein gap. Hit 130-140g/day total.

supplements.name: Vitamin D3
supplements.purpose: 32-40% of Canadians deficient; South Asian + Canadian winter = higher risk.
supplements.notes: Fat-soluble — take with breakfast containing fat (paneer, paratha, nuts).

supplements.name: Vitamin B12 (methylcobalamin)
supplements.purpose: ~62% of vegetarians have low B12. Dairy alone isn't enough.

supplements.name: Algal omega-3 (EPA+DHA)
supplements.purpose: Vegetarians convert <10% of ALA to EPA/DHA. Algal oil is the fix.
supplements.notes: Verify EPA+DHA totals on label — don't fall for products quoting only total omega-3 (mostly flax-ALA filler).

supplements.name: Electrolytes (Na/K/Mg)
supplements.purpose: Replace sodium + potassium lost in sweat during longer runs.
supplements.form: Powder mix (Liquid IV, LMNT, Nuun, Skratch)
supplements.notes: Skip on rest days or short walks (<30 min). Only useful when you're actually sweating meaningfully.

supplements.name: Caffeine (coffee or pill)
supplements.purpose: Strong evidence for endurance + sprint performance. 3-6 mg/kg pre-workout.
supplements.form: Coffee (~80 mg/cup) or anhydrous caffeine tablet
supplements.notes: HARD RULE: not after 2 PM. Sleep > workout boost. Skip rest days to preserve sensitivity.

supplements.name: Melatonin (low-dose)
supplements.purpose: Phase-shift daytime sleep + mild hypnotic. Low-dose mimics physiological levels.
supplements.notes: Use only on shift days or jet-lag-like situations. Higher doses (3-5 mg) cause morning grogginess and can blunt own melatonin.
supplements.evidence: Strong — Cochrane review (Liira 2014): +24 min daytime sleep duration for shift workers. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25113164/

supplements.name: Magnesium glycinate
supplements.purpose: Sleep onset/quality via GABA-A modulation. Glycinate form is gentle on GI.
supplements.notes: Especially helpful on shift days when sleep quality is compromised. Use bisglycinate, NOT oxide/citrate (those cause GI issues).
supplements.evidence: Moderate — Mah & Pitre 2021 meta-analysis: ~17 min faster sleep onset in adults with insomnia. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34883514/

supplements.name: L-theanine
supplements.purpose: Anxiolytic without sedation. Smooths caffeine arousal; helpful pre-sleep to dampen residual caffeine.
supplements.timing: 30 min pre-sleep OR with shift caffeine
supplements.altSchedule: Also pairs 1:1 or 2:1 with shift caffeine for smoother alertness without crash.
supplements.notes: Two uses: (1) pre-sleep on shift days, (2) mid-shift with caffeine. Don't sedate, no jitter.
supplements.evidence: Moderate — Williams 2020 systematic review: anxiolytic + attention benefits, especially with caffeine. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31758301/

supplements.name: Glycine
supplements.purpose: Drops core body temp pre-sleep — helpful sleeping in a warm room at 12:30 PM.
supplements.notes: Optional. Synthetic glycine is lacto-veg. Bannai 2012 small studies show subjective sleep improvement.
supplements.evidence: Weak-to-moderate — small Japanese RCTs (Yamadera, Bannai). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22529837/

weekly.name: Quarterly blood test
weekly.where: LifeLabs, Dynacare, or via GP requisition
weekly.costCAD: 30-80 per test out-of-pocket; covered if GP orders

shiftNote: Shift days use a flipped schedule. Caffeine cutoff is 5:30 AM (6h before 12:30 PM sleep). D3+omega3 with mid-shift meal (has fat). Creatine with wake-up. Whey with mid-shift OR post-workout (10 PM).
